title: buddleja salviifolia
intro: Buddleja salviifolia, also known as the butterfly bush, is a flowering plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Africa. The leaves and flowers of the plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Buddleja salviifolia include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating pain, as well as its potential use as an antacid and diuretic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
